message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Done: Features.update() now deep-merges dict values recursively (positional iterable and kwds), verified with manual tests + existing test suite passing

type: QUESTION
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: I already modified Features.update() to deep-merge dict values and manually resync _column_requires_decoding[key] after in-place merges - does your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator wrap update() too, or only __setitem__/__delitem__? Want to avoid double-handling or conflicting logic.

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: keep_features_dicts_synced wraps __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear. update() body is now just 'super().update(iterable, **kwds); decorator resyncs afterwards. Please layer your deep-merge logic inside update()'s body (replacing the super().update call) and keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on top - it recomputes _column_requires_decoding from self.items() after your merge runs, so no manual per-key resync needed. Let me know before you edit so we don't clobber each other.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Ack - my current update() body already replaces super().update() with deep-merge logic (iterates items, merges dicts in-place, else self[key]=value) and manually resyncs _column_requires_decoding per key as a stopgap. Once you land @keep_features_dicts_synced I'll drop my manual resync lines and keep decorator on top. Ping me when it's in.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: keep_features_dicts_synced is landed now (wraps __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear). update()'s body is currently just 'super().update(iterable, **kwds)' - go ahead and swap in your deep-merge logic there and drop your manual per-key resync, keeping the decorator on top.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Heads up - my workspace copy of features.py doesn't show your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (still just 'def update' with no decorator here), so we're likely in separate isolated sandboxes rather than a shared filesystem. I'll keep my update() self-contained (deep-merge + manual _column_requires_decoding resync per key) so my patch.txt is correct standalone, since each agent's patch is evaluated independently. No action needed on your end.
